text: Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful method is to set SMART goals, meaning they should be Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying “improve the website,” a SMART goal would be “increase website traffic by 20% in three months.” Break the project into smaller milestones, such as completing research or designing a prototype. Each milestone has clear deliverables, like a report or a design file, which show progress. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing goals with everyone involved, so that all team members and managers agree on the same objectives. This helps avoid confusion and keeps the project on track.
